You are a flag detection model for government RFP screening. Your job is to identify which flags, if any, are present in the provided RFP text chunk.

You will be given:
- A list of relevant flag definitions and examples (retrieved context)
- An RFP text chunk to analyze

Rules:
- Output one flag name per line, using only the exact flag names listed below
- If no flags are present, output: no_flag
- Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any other text
- Only detect a flag when the evidence in the chunk is explicit — do not infer from weak signals

Valid flags:
waterfall_methodology, off_the_shelf_software, no_custom_development, lpta_source_selection,
small_business_set_aside, 8a_set_aside, wosb_set_aside, edwosb_set_aside, sdvosb_set_aside,
hubzone_set_aside, agile_methodology, oral_presentation, design_exercise, budget_too_low,
brownfield, onsite_required, onsite_madison, large_team, marginal_short_duration, no_flag

[Retrieved context]

Example 1:
Component: FEMA Published Date: Previously 06/29/2022 Published On: NAICS: 541511 - Custom Computer Competition: YES Programming Services Contract Vehicle: NITAAC CIO-SP3 Small Business Program: WOSB Contract Status: New Requirement Anticipated Award Quarter: Q4 FY2022 Estimated Solicitation Release: 2022-07-13 Contract Complete: 2027-08-31

Example 2:
A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

Example 3:
This action will be a Firm Fixed-Price Contract to include software and associated success support with an estimated annual cost of $532,194 for a one (1) year base period with four (4) one-year option periods. The provider is a HUBZone small business set-aside directed to DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9), 161 Fort Evans Road NE, Suite 255 Leesburg VA 20176.

The solicitation proposes to award an Indefinite Quantity Contract (IQC) for:
• Posit Package Manager Advanced
• Posit Connect Advanced - 200 Licenses Package
• Posit Workbench Advanced - 20 Licenses Package
• Posit Success Support

DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9) is the only HUBZone small business source that is a Posit authorized distributor identified through market research that is capable of providing these part numbers. The contract will cover a Base Period of one year with four one year option periods for each item. The total estimated dollar value for the base year is $532,194.

Example 2:
5.1.1 Web-Based Reporting System: The contractor shall support and maintain a web-based project tracking system for approximately 400 active SERDP and ESTCP projects. The incumbent contractor currently maintains the SERDP and ESTCP Management System (SEMS), which is used to track technical and financial status of projects, contract actions, document status, financial planning, and distribution. In addition, the contractor shall maintain information from past projects since FY95 within SEMS and analyze information as needed. The contractor shall adhere to security plans for SEMS according to industry best practices. The contractor shall work with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) to ensure SEMS is in compliance with cybersecurity and other information management requirements. The contractor shall report findings that require action and provide recommendations of actions to the Authorizing Official. The contractor shall provide limited modifications as required by the Director to upgrade capability.

Example 3:
Project Scope: The selected vendor will be responsible for implementation of a Cloud-based/SaaS ERP/SIS solution to provide the following services: Must support all Core ERP/SIS functions including but not limited to: Financial Aid – Process Financial Aid Applications, Manage Need Analysis, Track Requirements, Complies with Federal regulations, Funds Management, Budgeting, Package and Award Aid, Disburse Aid, Process Loans, Return Title IV Funds, Manage Federal and Institutional Work Study, Transfer Monitoring, Monitor Satisfactory Academic Progress, Manage Communications, Maintain the College Financing Plan, Self-Service for Financial Aid. Degree Planning and Awarding – Course Catalogs, Requirements substitution and waiver processing. Student Accounts Receivables – Managing Student Accounts, Student Billing and Delinquencies, Manage Exemptions and Contracts, Title IV processing, Accounts Receivable, Collections/Aging, Cash Receipting, Self-Service for Student Accounts Receivable. Financial Information System – GL, Chart of Accounts, Budget Development, Personnel Budgeting, Purchasing, Purchase Orders, Receiving, Reporting, Accounts Payable, Fixed Assets, Research Accounting (Proposals and Grants), Cash Management, Self-Service for Financial Services. Select HRIS Features to Integrate with Paycom (HRIS). Must include data migration and implementation support and customizations. Describe what services you provide to help transition from our current on premise, Unidata version of Ellucian Colleague to a new SaaS solution.

---

RFP chunk to analyze:
DISA HC1047, DITCO National Capital Region. SE3 Service Life Cycle Support. Services within SE3 follow Service Life Cycle SLC processes where operations and support planning and execution are detailed. These processes are tailored and maintained by the individual program management office of each capability. Within these processes, configuration management (CM), release management (RM), and testing activities are used to track and verify the changes to the operational baselines and ensure the changes meet requirements. This task order provides support within SE3 for testing, CM, and RM. The initial strategy is to support the Strategic Knowledge Integration Web SKIWeb, Storage as a Service (STaaS), Enterprise Messaging (EM), and Enterprise Privileged User Access Service (EPAUS) programs. 541512 - Computer Systems Design Services. Follow On. HC1047. Soliel, LLC. 8(a) Sole Source. Single $650K - $4.99M. 5 years. Fort George G. Meade, MD. Secret. FY 2022 / QTR 4 - FY 2023 / QTR 2. Molinillo-Corral, Silvia.

Example 1:
To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Example 2:
Project Scope: The selected vendor will be responsible for implementation of a Cloud-based/SaaS ERP/SIS solution to provide the following services: Must support all Core ERP/SIS functions including but not limited to: Financial Aid – Process Financial Aid Applications, Manage Need Analysis, Track Requirements, Complies with Federal regulations, Funds Management, Budgeting, Package and Award Aid, Disburse Aid, Process Loans, Return Title IV Funds, Manage Federal and Institutional Work Study, Transfer Monitoring, Monitor Satisfactory Academic Progress, Manage Communications, Maintain the College Financing Plan, Self-Service for Financial Aid. Degree Planning and Awarding – Course Catalogs, Requirements substitution and waiver processing. Student Accounts Receivables – Managing Student Accounts, Student Billing and Delinquencies, Manage Exemptions and Contracts, Title IV processing, Accounts Receivable, Collections/Aging, Cash Receipting, Self-Service for Student Accounts Receivable. Financial Information System – GL, Chart of Accounts, Budget Development, Personnel Budgeting, Purchasing, Purchase Orders, Receiving, Reporting, Accounts Payable, Fixed Assets, Research Accounting (Proposals and Grants), Cash Management, Self-Service for Financial Services. Select HRIS Features to Integrate with Paycom (HRIS). Must include data migration and implementation support and customizations. Describe what services you provide to help transition from our current on premise, Unidata version of Ellucian Colleague to a new SaaS solution.

CESO requires contractor establish a modern SWS service operation as part of a state-of-the-art, highly automated platform capable of supporting a rapidly expanding customer population. CESO is seeking a service provider that can flawlessly take over existing IT services and seamlessly transition to a new commercial cloud environment with automated and integrated customer facing (front office) and non-customer facing (back office) capabilities. 541512 - Computer Systems Design Services. Example 2:
This is a COMBINED SYNOPSIS/SOLICITATION for commercial items prepared in accordance with the information in FAR Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with the additional information included in this notice. This solicitation is advertised as a 100% SDVOSB business set-aside. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; a written solicitation will not be issued. PAPER COPIES OF THIS SOLICITATION WILL NOT BE AVAILABLE. This combined synopsis/solicitation SHALL be posted to SAM.gov https://sam.gov/content/home). The RFQ number is 93310026Q0004. This solicitation documents and incorporates provisions and clauses in effect through FAC 2023-06 11/06/2023. It is the responsibility of the contractor to be familiar with the applicable clauses and provisions. The FAR clauses may be accessed in full text at these addresses http://www.acquisition.gov/far/. The NAICS code is 541519 and the Small Business Standard is $34 Million. The Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) intends to award a firm fixed price purchase order to procure zoom for government (ZFG) Licenses. Please see Attachment 1 – Vendor Quote Sheet for the details.

Example 3:
2.0 Task Order 01 Scope

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. The purpose is to achieve a clear and mutual understanding of contract requirements, establish communication channels, and set expectations for successful contract execution. Absolutely no changes to the contract will be made, nor contract terms renegotiated. The meeting will also serve as a forum for questions and clarifications.

2.1 Post Award Kickoff Conference

2.1.1 Each Contractor shall participate in a Post Award Kickoff Conference, to be held in Colorado Springs, CO no later than 30 days after contract award. The Post Award Kickoff Conference will be scheduled for approximately 90 minutes, conducted face-to-face, and will not include other contractors from the IDIQ contractor pool.

2.1.2 Each Contractor will be awarded a firm-fixed price TO valued at $10,000 to complete TO 01.

For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

5.1.1 Web-Based Reporting System: The contractor shall support and maintain a web-based project tracking system for approximately 400 active SERDP and ESTCP projects. The incumbent contractor currently maintains the SERDP and ESTCP Management System (SEMS), which is used to track technical and financial status of projects, contract actions, document status, financial planning, and distribution. In addition, the contractor shall maintain information from past projects since FY95 within SEMS and analyze information as needed. The contractor shall adhere to security plans for SEMS according to industry best practices. The contractor shall work with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) to ensure SEMS is in compliance with cybersecurity and other information management requirements. The contractor shall report findings that require action and provide recommendations of actions to the Authorizing Official. The contractor shall provide limited modifications as required by the Director to upgrade capability.

Example 3:
A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

The requirement is for continuity of IAS IT support services following a bridge contract. Services include:
- IAS System Operations (monitoring, administration, database support)
- IAS/IPP Help Desk Support
- IAS Releases, Upgrades, and Enhancements
- IAS Security and Program Management

Estimated (Base: 6 months + Option: 6 months)

Buchanan and Edwards is uniquely qualified due to:
- Continuity & Risk Mitigation: Retains critical IAS personnel (Program Manager, Functional Leads, Senior Developers) without costly and risky transition.
- Specialized Expertise: Proven experience with IAS SDLC, PRISM upgrades, Oracle DB migration, and USDA-specific interfaces (FMMI, IPP).
- Urgency & Mission Criticality: The current bridge contract awarded during a bid protest period is due to expire 2/28/2026. USDA and OCP have urgent requirements including but not limited to the second release and continued enhancement of the Requirements Planning Tool (RPT) 2.0, that necessitate the continuity of current IAS key personnel to ensure successful execution.

The provider is a HUBZone small business set-aside directed to DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9), 161 Fort Evans Road NE, Suite 255 Leesburg VA 20176.

The solicitation proposes to award an Indefinite Quantity Contract (IQC) for:
• Posit Package Manager Advanced
• Posit Connect Advanced - 200 Licenses Package
• Posit Workbench Advanced - 20 Licenses Package
• Posit Success Support

DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9) is the only HUBZone small business source that is a Posit authorized distributor identified through market research that is capable of providing these part numbers. The contract will cover a Base Period of one year with four one year option periods for each item. The total estimated dollar value for the base year is $532,194.

Example 2:
To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Example 3:
Project Scope: The selected vendor will be responsible for implementation of a Cloud-based/SaaS ERP/SIS solution to provide the following services: Must support all Core ERP/SIS functions including but not limited to: Financial Aid – Process Financial Aid Applications, Manage Need Analysis, Track Requirements, Complies with Federal regulations, Funds Management, Budgeting, Package and Award Aid, Disburse Aid, Process Loans, Return Title IV Funds, Manage Federal and Institutional Work Study, Transfer Monitoring, Monitor Satisfactory Academic Progress, Manage Communications, Maintain the College Financing Plan, Self-Service for Financial Aid. Degree Planning and Awarding – Course Catalogs, Requirements substitution and waiver processing. Student Accounts Receivables – Managing Student Accounts, Student Billing and Delinquencies, Manage Exemptions and Contracts, Title IV processing, Accounts Receivable, Collections/Aging, Cash Receipting, Self-Service for Student Accounts Receivable. Financial Information System – GL, Chart of Accounts, Budget Development, Personnel Budgeting, Purchasing, Purchase Orders, Receiving, Reporting, Accounts Payable, Fixed Assets, Research Accounting (Proposals and Grants), Cash Management, Self-Service for Financial Services. Select HRIS Features to Integrate with Paycom (HRIS). Must include data migration and implementation support and customizations. Describe what services you provide to help transition from our current on premise, Unidata version of Ellucian Colleague to a new SaaS solution.

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Example 2:
This action will be a Firm Fixed-Price Contract to include software and associated success support with an estimated annual cost of $532,194 for a one (1) year base period with four (4) one-year option periods. The provider is a HUBZone small business set-aside directed to DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9), 161 Fort Evans Road NE, Suite 255 Leesburg VA 20176.

The solicitation proposes to award an Indefinite Quantity Contract (IQC) for:
• Posit Package Manager Advanced
• Posit Connect Advanced - 200 Licenses Package
• Posit Workbench Advanced - 20 Licenses Package
• Posit Success Support

DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9) is the only HUBZone small business source that is a Posit authorized distributor identified through market research that is capable of providing these part numbers. The contract will cover a Base Period of one year with four one year option periods for each item. The total estimated dollar value for the base year is $532,194.

Example 3:
A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

5.1.1 Web-Based Reporting System: The contractor shall support and maintain a web-based project tracking system for approximately 400 active SERDP and ESTCP projects. The incumbent contractor currently maintains the SERDP and ESTCP Management System (SEMS), which is used to track technical and financial status of projects, contract actions, document status, financial planning, and distribution. In addition, the contractor shall maintain information from past projects since FY95 within SEMS and analyze information as needed. The contractor shall adhere to security plans for SEMS according to industry best practices. The contractor shall work with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) to ensure SEMS is in compliance with cybersecurity and other information management requirements. The contractor shall report findings that require action and provide recommendations of actions to the Authorizing Official. The contractor shall provide limited modifications as required by the Director to upgrade capability.

Degree Planning and Awarding – Course Catalogs, Requirements substitution and waiver processing. Select HRIS Features to Integrate with Paycom (HRIS). Must include data migration and implementation support and customizations. Describe what services you provide to help transition from our current on premise, Unidata version of Ellucian Colleague to a new SaaS solution.

A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

Example 3:
2.0 Task Order 01 Scope

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. The purpose is to achieve a clear and mutual understanding of contract requirements, establish communication channels, and set expectations for successful contract execution. Absolutely no changes to the contract will be made, nor contract terms renegotiated. The meeting will also serve as a forum for questions and clarifications.

2.1 Post Award Kickoff Conference

2.1.1 Each Contractor shall participate in a Post Award Kickoff Conference, to be held in Colorado Springs, CO no later than 30 days after contract award. The Post Award Kickoff Conference will be scheduled for approximately 90 minutes, conducted face-to-face, and will not include other contractors from the IDIQ contractor pool.

2.1.2 Each Contractor will be awarded a firm-fixed price TO valued at $10,000 to complete TO 01.

To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Solicitation Number: 1333ND25SNB340019
Agency: Department of Commerce, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service
Subject: Marine Observation Buoy Refurbishment and Deployment Services

The National Weather Service (NWS) is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified HUBZone certified small business concerns capable of performing refurbishment, calibration, and at-sea deployment of marine meteorological observation buoys along the Gulf Coast region. This sources sought notice is issued in contemplation of a HUBZone small business set-aside under FAR Subpart 19.13.

The anticipated requirement includes the overhaul and sensor recalibration of approximately 35 Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) stations and 12 moored buoy platforms annually, including hull cleaning, anti-fouling treatment, battery replacement, data logger programming, and vessel-based deployment. Work will be performed at a contractor-furnished facility within 100 miles of the Gulf Coast and at sea locations as directed by task order.

The applicable NAICS code is 336611 – Ship Building and Repairing, with a small business size standard of 1,300 employees. The estimated contract value is $6,200,000 over a base year and three option years.

Responses to this notice should include: (1) company name, address, CAGE code, and UEI number; (2) confirmation of active HUBZone certification with SBA certification number; (3) a brief capability statement not to exceed five pages; and (4) documentation of past performance on similar marine instrumentation or vessel-based deployment contracts.

If a sufficient number of qualified HUBZone small business concerns respond and the contracting officer determines there is a reasonable expectation that at least two HUBZone concerns will submit competitive proposals at fair market prices, NOAA intends to issue the formal solicitation as a total HUBZone set-aside with the FAR 52.219-3 clause and applicable HUBZone price evaluation preference under FAR 52.219-4. Interested firms must respond no later than June 20, 2025, at 2:00 PM Eastern Time.

(a) Definition. for the Department of Veterans Affairs, ''Service-disabled Veteran-owned small business concern or SDVOSB'':
(1) Means a small business concern—
(i) Not less than 51 percent of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or, in the case of any publicly owned business, not less than 51 percent of the stock of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or eligible surviving spouses...
(b) General. In order for a concern to submit an offer and be eligible for the award of an SDVOSB set-aside or sole source contract, the concern must qualify as a small business concern under the size standard corresponding to the NAICS code assigned to the contract and be listed as an SDVOSB participant in the SBA certification database as set forth in 13 CFR 128.
(1) Offers received from entities that are not certified SDVOSBs and listed in the SBA certification database at the time of offer shall not be considered.
(2) Any award resulting from this solicitation shall be made to a certified SDVOSB listed in the SBA certification database who is eligible at the time of submission of offer(s) and at the time of award.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The field-validated survey shall serve as the authoritative equipment inventory for LOTO procedure development.

Based on current facility records and prior inventories, the Contractor should anticipate development of LOTO procedures for no more than five hundred (500) pieces of equipment. If validated quantities materially exceed this estimate, scope adjustments shall be addressed through contract modification.

5.1.2 LOTO Procedure Development
The Contractor shall develop written, equipment-based LOTO procedures for each identified piece of equipment, including unique procedures and shared/common procedures where appropriate. Procedures shall comply with OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 and be developed consistent with ANSI/ASSE Z244.1 and NFPA 70E (current edition) and the VA written Compliance Program, See Attached, (138-9-2024 Lock-Out Tag-Out Policy).

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. Example 2:
6 Challenge-Based Acquisition
ClaimsCore will be procured through a multi-phase challenge process. The objective is to create an environment where acquisition decisions are based on experimentation and demonstration of existing or emerging technology through a rapid and impactful assessment of commercial solutions and competing "prototypes" (i.e., limited implementations).

CMS contemplates multiple awards including a few contracts – perhaps as many as four or perhaps only one. Due to the operational cost and burden of a PoC, fewer awards are preferred. Accordingly, CMS has established an aggressive base-period (POC Phase) affordability objective. CMS encourages bidders to propose the best Proof of Concept solution possible at the lowest possible cost. Those vendors will begin work under a proof-of-concept (POC) phase where their solutions are tested against real CMS data and measured against CMS-established success criteria and metrics.

After the POC phase, CMS anticipates the down-select of contractors by not exercising option(s) for further work based on unilateral determination of CMS' best interest. This approach ensures solutions are validated in practice, stakeholder confidence is built incrementally, and only Contractors with proven capability at scale advance.

Example 3:
The requirement is for continuity of IAS IT support services following a bridge contract. Services include:
- IAS System Operations (monitoring, administration, database support)
- IAS/IPP Help Desk Support
- IAS Releases, Upgrades, and Enhancements
- IAS Security and Program Management

Estimated (Base: 6 months + Option: 6 months)

Buchanan and Edwards is uniquely qualified due to:
- Continuity & Risk Mitigation: Retains critical IAS personnel (Program Manager, Functional Leads, Senior Developers) without costly and risky transition.
- Specialized Expertise: Proven experience with IAS SDLC, PRISM upgrades, Oracle DB migration, and USDA-specific interfaces (FMMI, IPP).
- Urgency & Mission Criticality: The current bridge contract awarded during a bid protest period is due to expire 2/28/2026. USDA and OCP have urgent requirements including but not limited to the second release and continued enhancement of the Requirements Planning Tool (RPT) 2.0, that necessitate the continuity of current IAS key personnel to ensure successful execution.

The Fly-Off will consist of several phases:
a. Solicitation Phase (specific Call procedures are explained in Section 7)
i. Pitch-Deck Submission and Evaluation (which includes an eligibility determination)
ii. Request for Proposal
iii. Proposal Evaluation
b. Contract Award
i. Negotiation

i. Adjudication of any required modifications to base Fly-Off test plan based on specific system under test.
d. System Integration onto Autonomy Prime Testbed Aircraft
i. Integration of test subject hardware + software into Autonomy Prime Testbed (Interface Control Document (ICD) and Application Program Interface (API) will be provided during RFP phase of solicitation) at Eglin AFB, FL.
ii. Ground Checks of the system completed.
e. Flight test events
i. The Autonomy Prime testbed will attempt to fly predetermined course(s) using navigation solution(s) provided by the test system. Divergence from optimal course will be allowed and monitored up to a predetermined geofence. Error from optimal course will be measured.
f. System Disassembly from Autonomy Prime Testbed Aircraft
i. Test subject hardware + software should be removed from the Autonomy Prime Testbed and the Testbed returned to its original configuration.
ii. Inspection by the USAF test team to ensure the Testbed configuration has returned to its baseline and all performer assets have been returned.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

RFP chunk to analyze:
Q2:  Will the Government accept a migration plan submitted as an appendix to the slide presentation and that the migration services strategy plan be evaluated as part of the proposal?

A2:  No.  The offeror must present its approach to integrate with existing DISA solutions and provide the migration services (outlined in PWS section 6.1.8) as part of their oral presentation.  

Q3: Can offerors submit external handouts for network diagrams outside of their slide presentation and not count against the maximum slide count? 

A3: No. The offeror must present the network design as part of their oral proposal.  The offeror may include network diagrams of the proposed design within the presentation slides as visual aids.  These designs will count toward the maximum slide count. 

Q4: Would DISA consider Oral and Technical Proposal submitted separately? Meaning, can Orals be assigned once down selected? 

A4: No. Also, there is no down selection process outlined in the RFP.

Q5: Will the slides for the Technical and Management Approach (Volume II) be due at the same time as Price volume and other elements of the full proposal?

A5: Yes; see the addendum to FAR 52.212-1(a)(7)(vi), Electronic Offers.  The offeror must submit all volumes with their proposal. 

Q6: How will offerors schedule / be notified of their Oral Presentation date and time? 

A6: See addendum to 52.212-1(a)(7)(vii)(B)(7). The Government intends to schedule oral proposals with responsive offerors approximately 10 business days after the solicitation closes. Oral proposals will take place approximately within 30 days after they are scheduled; dates and times will be assigned at random for all responsive offerors.

Q7: Are all offerors given an opportunity to present their technical approach orally via MS Teams following timely and accurate submission of the proposal?

A7: If an offeror's proposal is timely and complete (adheres to the RFP), the Government will invite the offeror to present their technical proposal orally.

1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Q23: Would the Government consider allowing the submission of a written technical proposal in support of or in lieu of an oral presentation?

A23: No.

(B) Oral Presentation Medium: The Government intends to hold oral presentations virtually via a Microsoft Teams meeting. Offerors are not required to purchase any VTC software in response to this solicitation and will not be reimbursed for any costs associated. Offerors must state in their submission whether they can or cannot present their oral proposal via Microsoft Teams. If an offeror cannot present their oral proposal via Microsoft Teams, the Government will make every reasonable attempt to accommodate the offeror via another video teleconferencing (VTC) medium or voice-only telephone. By participating in the oral presentations, the offeror acknowledges that it complies with all solicitation rules and parameters, in accordance with applicable laws and statutes.

(C) Recording Oral Presentations. Recording oral presentations by offerors is strictly prohibited, notwithstanding local laws and regulations with regards to virtual meetings or voice-only telephone oral presentations. The Government reserves the right to record oral presentations. If recorded, the recording is source selection sensitive and will be handled accordingly.

(D) Exchanges During Oral Presentation. The Government intends to engage in interactive dialogue during the oral presentations. These exchanges are viewed as a component of the oral presentation itself and do not constitute discussions.

3. Oral Proposal. Oral proposals are limited to a maximum of 60 minutes per offeror. At the conclusion of the oral proposal, the Government will convene for up to a 30-minute internal caucus to determine if there are any questions for the offeror. Example 3:
7 Proof of Concept Scope
During the challenge, contractor solutions will be run in parallel with CMS's legacy shared systems. This head-to-head approach ensures that performance, accuracy, and reliability can be directly measured against current operations. Contractors must demonstrate that their platforms can adjudicate Part A, Part B and DME claims in near real-time, deliver outputs consistent with legacy results, and provide transparent explanations for any variances. Success will be evaluated based on clear, measurable metrics defined in the POC Metrics table and QASP including accuracy, throughput, interoperability, and stakeholder satisfaction. CMS anticipates that only one solution will advance to production.

PoC scope may include, but is not limited to:
1. Adjudicating large claim volumes quickly and accurately. Accuracy will be measured through both CMS and contractor tools that compare adjudication outcomes and artifacts with the legacy system.
2. Successfully integrating with Tier 1 Systems as described below.
3. Demonstrating user interfaces and operational workflows (including both end-user and administrative functions) relevant to the PoC claim scope including any pre-configured Original Medicare rule sets, workflows, or configuration.
4. Demonstrate the 276/277 X12 transaction flow is happening accurately and in near real-time.

RFP chunk to analyze:
a. Subfactor 1: Offerors must brief a network design showcasing their approach to designing an enterprise network supporting multiple data centers as well as cloud environments utilizing the proposed solutions to meet the requirements of PWS Section 6.1 and PWS Appendix 5. The purpose of the network design is to demonstrate the offeror's approach to the proposed solution within the context of a Data Center network. The offeror may include network diagrams of the proposed design within the presentation slides as visual aids. Offerors may reference manufacturers' published data sheets for network components to demonstrate how solutions are meeting the requirements of PWS Appendix 5. The offeror must include the OEM data sheets for the devices used in the network design; the data sheets must be included as attachments/handouts to the presentation. The network design must use the criteria below.

(1) Design a network for two data centers, each with the following requirements:
(a) Traditionally hosted compute:
(i) Support for 1000 physical hosts per data center.
(ii) Support for 3000 virtual hosts.
(iii) Support 10Gbps throughput per host interface.
(iv) Future support of up to 100Gbps per host interface.
(v) Logical separation and control of Production and Management with further separation of Web, Database, App, Infrastructure Management, Enterprise Services, Data Replication, and Backup functions.
(vi) Solution must support micro segmentation and Zero Trust.
(vii) Support for fully architected enclaves requiring only external connectivity.
(2) Provide secure connectivity between two data centers.
(3) Provide secure connectivity between each data center and one or more commercial cloud environments.

1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. Absolutely no changes to the contract will be made, nor contract terms renegotiated. The meeting will also serve as a forum for questions and clarifications.

2.1.2 Each Contractor will be awarded a firm-fixed price TO valued at $10,000 to complete TO 01.

Example 3:
PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

The requirement is for continuity of IAS IT support services following a bridge contract. Services include:
- IAS System Operations (monitoring, administration, database support)
- IAS/IPP Help Desk Support
- IAS Releases, Upgrades, and Enhancements
- IAS Security and Program Management

Estimated (Base: 6 months + Option: 6 months)

Buchanan and Edwards is uniquely qualified due to:
- Continuity & Risk Mitigation: Retains critical IAS personnel (Program Manager, Functional Leads, Senior Developers) without costly and risky transition.
- Specialized Expertise: Proven experience with IAS SDLC, PRISM upgrades, Oracle DB migration, and USDA-specific interfaces (FMMI, IPP).
- Urgency & Mission Criticality: The current bridge contract awarded during a bid protest period is due to expire 2/28/2026. USDA and OCP have urgent requirements including but not limited to the second release and continued enhancement of the Requirements Planning Tool (RPT) 2.0, that necessitate the continuity of current IAS key personnel to ensure successful execution.

Example 2:
Requirements – Technical: The selected software must meet the following criteria: Content Management System – preference will be given to a mature, proven well-known CMS that supports multiple secure user accounts that enable specific view/report/add/edit permissions. Software should be reliable, secure and robust, in accordance with industry standards and practices. The software must be cloud based (software as a service) platform. Ability to integrate with other software solutions via an API or other methods that would allow the potential for CRM and Web Site interfaces. Software will contain an accurate and complete audit trail that ensures efficient processing. Software must be able to identify and itemize all fees; such as program, rental, transaction, and convenience fees. Must have the ability to provide total program costs to client. Software must be compatible with the County's financial software, Tyler Munis, and post transactions to the General Ledger. Software shall include the ability to implement different security access levels, and elevated admin privileges to appropriate staff. Launch Live Date – Targeted for July 1, 2026. Software must have a comprehensive storage procedure to store all documents and records in a manner that is easily retrievable by County. Software must have cybersecurity protections and company must provide cyberliability insurance coverage.

Example 3:
The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. Automated testing, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines will be central to this framework, enabling quick iterations and reducing certification and Authorization to Operate (ATO) delays. By aligning with modern software practices, Case Development Modernization (CDM) will ensure that value is delivered early and continuously throughout the lifecycle. CDM delivery includes, but is not limited to, the following activities and responsibilities:
• Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

RFP chunk to analyze:
Insurance Service requires an agile development program to collaborate with its business subject matter experts to develop enhancements, increase automation, implement AI, improve processing with added efficiencies, and integrate with both internal and external systems.

Insurance Service requires a sustainment program and service level agreement to ensure optimal system availability and performance during business hours, prompt resolution of reported help desk incidents and requests, as well as reliable after-hours operations to execute and monitor daily cycle processing, batch processing, and data transmissions with external interface partners to ensure successful overnight processing. VA also requires that this solution be interoperable and compatible with VA Insurance's existing platform.

The period of performance for this proposed action shall consist of a 12-month base period, with 2 12-month option periods and 6 optional tasks to provide additional sustainment support, additional development support, base product enhancements, system expansion, cloud migration and transition, if required.

5.0 SPECIFIC TASKS AND DELIVERABLES

The Contractor shall use an Agile DevSecOps approach to continuously deliver value across the LIPAS/VISION product to provide greater user visibility, clearly defined technical infrastructure, improved capabilities, as well as information security and accessibility as directed by VA Stakeholders. The Contractor shall provide expertise and support for Products and Software. The Contractor shall provide product performance management and Agile program management to improve quality and performance of features and value delivery. The Contractor shall provide support for centralized OIT intake and management of business and technical needs and requirements. The Contractor shall provide architecture support and design reviews to support operational value streams and all activities that span product objectives.

The Contractor shall comply with IT Development Security Operations (DevSecOps) methodologies for LIPAS in accordance with the working principles and definitions provided in Section 5.0. The Contractor shall provide dedicated teams of cross-functional staff to operate, maintain, and enhance the LIPAS/VISION platform and all associated interfaces with capabilities to streamline systems, improve scalability, increase availability of services, and enable more efficient delivery.

5.3 AGILE DEVELOPMENT AND ENHANCEMENT SCRUM TEAM

The contractor shall provide VA with integration of Agile software development and continuous integration / continuous deployment (CI/CD). The contractor shall support the intake of business needs, prioritization, and delivery. The contractor shall support and oversee initiatives and epics through initial business need definition, prioritization, and execution. The Contractor shall provide overarching architecture support. The contractor shall identify dependencies, inputs, and outputs, the timeline for completion of integrated efforts, and processes to manage the overall integrated effort. The Contractor shall adhere to the Agile Center of Excellence (ACOE) PLM Transformation Playbook and adopt modern practices to continuously improve VA experiences through a combination of people, processes, and technology as referenced in Section 2.0, the applicable documents section of this PWS.

The Contractor shall provide Agile Development Teams to enable DevSecOps Product support. The Contractor shall apply an Agile scrum approach and provide cross functional Agile teams for the development and enhancements of LIPAS and the underlying infrastructures in alignment with VA's Veteran-focused Integration Process (VIP) Guide and ACOE PLM Transformation Playbook. The Contractor shall drive and enable innovation, emphasize and promote delivery, open communication, team self-organization, and stakeholder participation.

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Example 1:
The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. Automated testing, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines will be central to this framework, enabling quick iterations and reducing certification and Authorization to Operate (ATO) delays. By aligning with modern software practices, Case Development Modernization (CDM) will ensure that value is delivered early and continuously throughout the lifecycle. CDM delivery includes, but is not limited to, the following activities and responsibilities:
• Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

Example 2:
7 Proof of Concept Scope
During the challenge, contractor solutions will be run in parallel with CMS's legacy shared systems. This head-to-head approach ensures that performance, accuracy, and reliability can be directly measured against current operations. Contractors must demonstrate that their platforms can adjudicate Part A, Part B and DME claims in near real-time, deliver outputs consistent with legacy results, and provide transparent explanations for any variances. Success will be evaluated based on clear, measurable metrics defined in the POC Metrics table and QASP including accuracy, throughput, interoperability, and stakeholder satisfaction. CMS anticipates that only one solution will advance to production.

PoC scope may include, but is not limited to:
1. Adjudicating large claim volumes quickly and accurately. Accuracy will be measured through both CMS and contractor tools that compare adjudication outcomes and artifacts with the legacy system.
2. Successfully integrating with Tier 1 Systems as described below.
3. Demonstrating user interfaces and operational workflows (including both end-user and administrative functions) relevant to the PoC claim scope including any pre-configured Original Medicare rule sets, workflows, or configuration.
4. Demonstrate the 276/277 X12 transaction flow is happening accurately and in near real-time.

Example 3:
How does this acquisition approach map to your Agile development approach and what incentives can the government provide to create more technical and schedule efficiencies?

DSCA is implementing SAFe agile methodology and is aligned to facilitate governance and decision making within DSCA and major IA stakeholders as per the established DSCA IT governance process.

A modern, agile product development methodology is required, with a strong emphasis on Human-Centered Design (HCD). The goal is to re-engineer and simplify processes, not just perform a "lift and shift."

Example 1:
How does this acquisition approach map to your Agile development approach and what incentives can the government provide to create more technical and schedule efficiencies?

DSCA is implementing SAFe agile methodology and is aligned to facilitate governance and decision making within DSCA and major IA stakeholders as per the established DSCA IT governance process.

A modern, agile product development methodology is required, with a strong emphasis on Human-Centered Design (HCD). The goal is to re-engineer and simplify processes, not just perform a "lift and shift."

Example 2:
7 Proof of Concept Scope
During the challenge, contractor solutions will be run in parallel with CMS's legacy shared systems. This head-to-head approach ensures that performance, accuracy, and reliability can be directly measured against current operations. Contractors must demonstrate that their platforms can adjudicate Part A, Part B and DME claims in near real-time, deliver outputs consistent with legacy results, and provide transparent explanations for any variances. Success will be evaluated based on clear, measurable metrics defined in the POC Metrics table and QASP including accuracy, throughput, interoperability, and stakeholder satisfaction. CMS anticipates that only one solution will advance to production.

PoC scope may include, but is not limited to:
1. Adjudicating large claim volumes quickly and accurately. Accuracy will be measured through both CMS and contractor tools that compare adjudication outcomes and artifacts with the legacy system.
2. Successfully integrating with Tier 1 Systems as described below.
3. Demonstrating user interfaces and operational workflows (including both end-user and administrative functions) relevant to the PoC claim scope including any pre-configured Original Medicare rule sets, workflows, or configuration.
4. Demonstrate the 276/277 X12 transaction flow is happening accurately and in near real-time.

Example 3:
5.1.1 Web-Based Reporting System: The contractor shall support and maintain a web-based project tracking system for approximately 400 active SERDP and ESTCP projects. The incumbent contractor currently maintains the SERDP and ESTCP Management System (SEMS), which is used to track technical and financial status of projects, contract actions, document status, financial planning, and distribution. In addition, the contractor shall maintain information from past projects since FY95 within SEMS and analyze information as needed. The contractor shall adhere to security plans for SEMS according to industry best practices. The contractor shall work with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) to ensure SEMS is in compliance with cybersecurity and other information management requirements. The contractor shall report findings that require action and provide recommendations of actions to the Authorizing Official. The contractor shall provide limited modifications as required by the Director to upgrade capability.

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

RFP chunk to analyze:
SPECIAL NOTICE FOR SB SET-ASIDE:
This RFP is a total small business set-aside. Offerors are strongly encouraged to ensure that the offeror is a small business based on the size standard for the NAICS Code assigned to this RFP. Reference 13 CFR Part 121, and the clauses and relevant FAR references contained within this solicitation. Ensure that your firm fits the definition including but not limited to any affiliations (domestic or foreign). The Small Business Administration determines whether an entity qualifies as a small business concern by counting its receipts, employees, or other measures including those of all its domestic and foreign affiliates, regardless of whether the affiliates are organized for profit. 13 C.F.R. 121.103(a)(6). SBA has a specific set of rules that explain when another person, business or entity is considered an affiliate for size standard purposes. SBA's rules on affiliation for its programs (except SBIR and STTR) are found at 13 C.F.R. 121.103.

4. EVALUATION FACTORS
As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The CSOW will include at a minimum a narrative addressing each paragraph in the Statement of Objectives (SOO) (specifically Paragraphs 3-6.12). The narrative will outline the Contractor's complete understanding of the requirement and how the contractor will fulfill all requirements.

The Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW) will be incorporated into the contract at the time of award. See the SOO, for the technical aspects.

Example 2:
PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

M.1.1 The Government intends to award 100% Small Business Set-aside, Firm Fixed Price, Level of Effort (FFP/LOE) contract. An award will be made to the offeror whose proposal represents the best value to the Government in accordance with the criteria set forth below, and is eligible for award.
M.1.1.1 There are two (2) evaluation factors:
Volume 1 Staffing Plan Factor
Volume 2 Price Factor
The relative order of importance of these Factors are described in Section M.4 below.
M.1.1.2 Proposal selection will be made utilizing the source selection trade-off process, considering the evaluation Factors and their relative order of importance (see Section M.4), to identify the proposal which represents the best value and is most advantageous to the Government. The Government will weigh the evaluation factors (other than the Cost/Price Factor) against the final evaluated price to the Government. As part of the best value determination, the relative strengths, weaknesses, and risks of each proposal shall be considered in selecting the offer that is most advantageous and represents the best overall value to the Government.

1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

Example 3:
2.0 Task Order 01 Scope

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. The purpose is to achieve a clear and mutual understanding of contract requirements, establish communication channels, and set expectations for successful contract execution. Absolutely no changes to the contract will be made, nor contract terms renegotiated. The meeting will also serve as a forum for questions and clarifications.

2.1 Post Award Kickoff Conference

2.1.1 Each Contractor shall participate in a Post Award Kickoff Conference, to be held in Colorado Springs, CO no later than 30 days after contract award. The Post Award Kickoff Conference will be scheduled for approximately 90 minutes, conducted face-to-face, and will not include other contractors from the IDIQ contractor pool.

2.1.2 Each Contractor will be awarded a firm-fixed price TO valued at $10,000 to complete TO 01.

A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.
