52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting (Oct 2022)

(a) This clause does not apply to the unrestricted portion of a partial set-aside.
(b) Definition. Similarly situated entity, as used in this clause, means a first-tier subcontractor, including an independent contractor, that-
(1) Has the same small business program status as that which qualified the prime contractor for the award (e.g., for a small business set-aside contract, any small business concern, without regard to its socioeconomic status); and
(2) Is considered small for the size standard under the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code the prime contractor assigned to the subcontract.
(c) Applicability. This clause applies only to-
(1) Contracts that have been set aside for any of the small business concerns identified in 19.000(a)(3);
(2) Part or parts of a multiple-award contract that have been set aside for any of the small business concerns identified in 19.000(a)(3);
(3) Contracts that have been awarded on a sole-source basis in accordance with subparts 19.8, 19.13, 19.14, and 19.15;
(6) Contracts using the HUBZone price evaluation preference to award to a HUBZone small business concern unless the concern waived the evaluation preference.

Example 1:
1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

Example 2:
Key Roles

Listed are the key roles the contractor shall provide at time of contract award and the general functionality of their position is listed in Appendix G. The minimum education, training, and experience required by contractor personnel (whether prime or subcontractor) to perform support tasks identified in this contract are defined in the labor category descriptions provided herein.

Additional information on these categories is found in Appendix G. Personnel in labor categories designated as key roles cannot be replaced without the approval of the government. The following are identified as key roles:

Program Manager
Systems Engineer
Facilities Manager
Security Manager

Beyond these key roles, non-key personnel categories include: Telecommunications Engineer, Cybersecurity Engineer, Systems Administrator, Information System Security Engineer (ISSE), Administrative Assistant, CAD Technician, Radio Frequency Systems Technologist, Information System Security Officer (ISSO), Logistician, Business Process Manager, Purchasing and Materials Coordinator, Project Manager, Configuration Manager, Information Technology Architect, Antenna Specialist, and Software Developer.

Example 3:
To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

5.1.1 Web-Based Reporting System: The contractor shall support and maintain a web-based project tracking system for approximately 400 active SERDP and ESTCP projects. The incumbent contractor currently maintains the SERDP and ESTCP Management System (SEMS), which is used to track technical and financial status of projects, contract actions, document status, financial planning, and distribution. In addition, the contractor shall maintain information from past projects since FY95 within SEMS and analyze information as needed. The contractor shall adhere to security plans for SEMS according to industry best practices. The contractor shall work with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) to ensure SEMS is in compliance with cybersecurity and other information management requirements. The contractor shall report findings that require action and provide recommendations of actions to the Authorizing Official. The contractor shall provide limited modifications as required by the Director to upgrade capability.

Example 2:
Key Roles

Listed are the key roles the contractor shall provide at time of contract award and the general functionality of their position is listed in Appendix G. The minimum education, training, and experience required by contractor personnel (whether prime or subcontractor) to perform support tasks identified in this contract are defined in the labor category descriptions provided herein.

Additional information on these categories is found in Appendix G. Personnel in labor categories designated as key roles cannot be replaced without the approval of the government. The following are identified as key roles:

Program Manager
Systems Engineer
Facilities Manager
Security Manager

Beyond these key roles, non-key personnel categories include: Telecommunications Engineer, Cybersecurity Engineer, Systems Administrator, Information System Security Engineer (ISSE), Administrative Assistant, CAD Technician, Radio Frequency Systems Technologist, Information System Security Officer (ISSO), Logistician, Business Process Manager, Purchasing and Materials Coordinator, Project Manager, Configuration Manager, Information Technology Architect, Antenna Specialist, and Software Developer.

Example 3:
To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

The Contractor will provide a written response outlining its technical solution to execute the requirement outlined in each paragraph and subparagraph in the SOO. "Yes," "Met," and other ambiguous responses that do not provide an actual technical solution will be deemed unacceptable. The NAFI will evaluate the CSOW as part of Technical Evaluation (Evaluation Factors - LPTA). The CSOW will be incorporated into the contract award.

G.3.1 Ordering Process
All ProTech-SBEM contract holders with the relevant Functional Categories awarded on their IDIQ contracts will be provided a fair opportunity to be considered on task orders, in accordance with FAR 16.507-2, unless exempted in accordance with applicable terms of the FAR.

Contractors are only eligible to compete for Task Orders issued under the specific Functional Categories for which they were evaluated (technical and price) and granted an award at the IDIQ level. An awardee's IDIQ contract shall explicitly identify the Functional Categories for which they are qualified.

The OCO, in consultation with the TO COR or other designated technical representatives, will examine the capabilities of the contract holders in order to determine if a small business (or socio-economic category) set-aside is appropriate for each order. After completing this examination, the OCO may limit competition for an order for small businesses. The OCO will then solicit proposals from small business contractors, as applicable, or open the competition to all contract holders after review and coordination with the NOAA Small Business Office and the Small Business Administration (SBA).

G.3.2 Small Business Reserve
This reserve is conducted in accordance with 13 CFR 125.2(e)(4) and therefore, if there are two or more contract awards to any one type of small business concern, the Government may compete any orders solely amongst the specified types of small business concerns if the "rule of two" or an alternative set-aside requirement provided in the small business program have been met.

1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

4. EVALUATION FACTORS
As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The CSOW will include at a minimum a narrative addressing each paragraph in the Statement of Objectives (SOO) (specifically Paragraphs 3-6.12). The narrative will outline the Contractor's complete understanding of the requirement and how the contractor will fulfill all requirements.

The Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW) will be incorporated into the contract at the time of award. See the SOO, for the technical aspects.

Example 2:
PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

10. FILE THREE – TECHNICAL SOLUTION (Statement of Objectives (SOO) & Evaluation Factors - LPTA for Award)

10.2. Technical Solution/CSOW
10.2.1. The Contractor will provide a CSOW. The CSOW defines/explains how the contractor will meet the tasks required to fulfill the requirements of the Statement of Objectives (SOO). The Contractor will provide a written response outlining its technical solution to execute the requirement outlined in each paragraph and subparagraph in the SOO. "Yes," "Met," and other ambiguous responses that do not provide an actual technical solution will be deemed unacceptable. The NAFI will evaluate the CSOW as part of Technical Evaluation (Evaluation Factors - LPTA). The CSOW will be incorporated into the contract award.

c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ)
Solicitation No.: EPA-R8-2026-RFQ-0112
Region 8 – Denver, Colorado

Section 1.0 – Background and Purpose

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), Region 8 Office of Communities, Tribes, and Environmental Assessment, requires contractor support to conduct a limited environmental justice (EJ) screening analysis and community engagement summary for three census tracts adjacent to the Portland Avenue Superfund site in Billings, Montana. This effort will supplement EPA's ongoing Community Involvement Plan (CIP) update and will inform future technical assistance planning under Executive Order 14008.

Section 2.0 – Scope of Work

The contractor shall utilize EJScreen and supplemental demographic data sources to prepare a screening report, develop a plain-language community summary document in English and Spanish, and facilitate one (1) virtual community listening session. All deliverables must comply with EPA's Information Quality Guidelines and Section 508 accessibility standards.

Section 3.0 – Estimated Price Range and Funding

3.1 The total estimated value of this requirement is between $18,500 and $24,000. This amount represents the total contract value inclusive of all direct costs, indirect costs, and profit/fee.

3.2 Funding for this effort is provided through EPA's Environmental Justice Collaborative Problem-Solving (EJCPS) program allocation for FY2026. No option periods are contemplated.

3.3 This acquisition will be conducted under simplified acquisition procedures in accordance with FAR Part 13. Quotations shall be evaluated on a best-value basis considering technical capability, relevant past performance, and price.

3.4 Interested vendors must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov) at the time of quote submission. The anticipated period of performance is approximately four (4) months from date of award.

AMENDMENT OF SOLICITATION / MODIFICATION OF CONTRACT
Contract No.: W912HZ-25-P-0044
Agency: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Savannah District
Effective Date: February 3, 2025

SECTION C – STATEMENT OF WORK

C.1 BACKGROUND
The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Savannah District, requires professional land surveying services to support ongoing civil works projects at Fort Stewart Military Installation, Hinesville, Georgia. Current installation mapping data for a specific 85-acre tract along the Canoochee River corridor requires updating to reflect recent construction activities, boundary adjustments, and topographic changes resulting from flood mitigation earthwork.

C.2 SCOPE
The contractor shall perform a boundary and topographic survey of the designated 85-acre parcel, produce a georeferenced CAD drawing and associated digital terrain model, and provide certified survey plats signed by a Georgia-licensed Professional Land Surveyor. All work shall conform to the minimum technical standards set forth in the Georgia Minimum Standards for Property Surveys (Rules 180-7-.04 and .05) and USACE EM 1110-1-1005 (Topographic Surveying).

C.3 TOTAL OBLIGATION AMOUNT
The total obligation amount for this purchase order is $22,750.00 (Twenty-Two Thousand Seven Hundred Fifty Dollars). This amount constitutes the maximum Government liability and includes all costs for labor, equipment, travel, field supplies, data processing, and deliverable production. No additional funds will be obligated.

C.4 DELIVERABLES
(a) Boundary survey plat (PDF and hard copy, 2 sets) – 30 days ARO
(b) Topographic survey CAD file (.dwg format, NAD83/NAVD88) – 30 days ARO
(c) Digital terrain model – 30 days ARO
(d) Final certified survey report – 45 days ARO

C.5 INSPECTION AND ACCEPTANCE
Inspection and acceptance shall be performed by the designated Contracting Officer's Representative (COR) in accordance with FAR 52.246-4, Inspection of Services – Fixed Price.

Example 1:
The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. Automated testing, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines will be central to this framework, enabling quick iterations and reducing certification and Authorization to Operate (ATO) delays. By aligning with modern software practices, Case Development Modernization (CDM) will ensure that value is delivered early and continuously throughout the lifecycle. CDM delivery includes, but is not limited to, the following activities and responsibilities:
• Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

6 Challenge-Based Acquisition
ClaimsCore will be procured through a multi-phase challenge process. The objective is to create an environment where acquisition decisions are based on experimentation and demonstration of existing or emerging technology through a rapid and impactful assessment of commercial solutions and competing "prototypes" (i.e., limited implementations).

CMS contemplates multiple awards including a few contracts – perhaps as many as four or perhaps only one. Due to the operational cost and burden of a PoC, fewer awards are preferred. Accordingly, CMS has established an aggressive base-period (POC Phase) affordability objective. CMS encourages bidders to propose the best Proof of Concept solution possible at the lowest possible cost. Those vendors will begin work under a proof-of-concept (POC) phase where their solutions are tested against real CMS data and measured against CMS-established success criteria and metrics.

After the POC phase, CMS anticipates the down-select of contractors by not exercising option(s) for further work based on unilateral determination of CMS' best interest. This approach ensures solutions are validated in practice, stakeholder confidence is built incrementally, and only Contractors with proven capability at scale advance.

RFP chunk to analyze:
THE NGA SOFTWARE WAY ELEMENTS

To consistently track these metrics and realize the above intent, the rest of this document provides a set of elements NGA and contractor teams must meet while delivering software products. These metrics and elements, collectively referred to as "The NGA Software Way," ensure each software product has the people, processes, and tools in place to effectively operate at NGA. They apply to any team building a new product or modifying one already in service, whether making small changes, adding large new features, or creating entirely new products. The NGA Software Way applies to software products of all phases, including existing software products across the agency (not simply new products). Except where noted below, these elements must also be used when evaluating and implementing commercial software products for use at NGA. We understand that for large, existing software products, implementing The NGA Software Way won't happen all at once; it should start with tracking the metrics above and iteratively meeting each of the elements below.

3. Prioritize Rapidly Delivering Value to Users

Once user needs are deeply understood, the team must decide which of those needs to prioritize. To do this, the team must define a minimum viable product (MVP), either in written form or, ideally, as a set of wireframes, and then share it with key stakeholders, customers, and users. The MVP is the smallest actual deliverable that can be reviewed by real customers to get feedback on if it solves a need and to test assumptions, learn quickly, and decrease risk upfront. The product manager must break down the MVP into actionable work items that are easily understood by the entire team. Teams must gather feedback as early as possible using mockups or interactive prototypes. As feedback is gathered, the prototype's fidelity should mature, and the team must transition to testing with the real product.

Each day a product isn't actively solving user needs in production increases the risk the team is building the wrong thing. Teams must prioritize rapidly getting a product to actual users in production to learn what works and how to successfully iterate; this first capability must be delivered in months, not years.

RFP chunk to analyze:
2.0 BACKGROUND

NGA has a mission-critical need for implementing the Automated Feature Extraction (AFE) to improve efficiency and effectiveness in mission data feature collection. FG Analysts spend extensive time on repetitive manual feature extraction, conflation, and validation, which are time-consuming activities and can potentially be automated. Implementing AFE as part of FG mission operations addresses these challenges and supports the NGA's 2035 GEOINT Concept of Operations. NGA envisions this initiative to leverage the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) to transform geospatial intelligence production, significantly reducing manual processing time, increasing data accuracy, and enabling the rapid delivery of intelligence to decision-makers.

This RFI seeks industry input on available out-of-the-box AFE capabilities and how they can be provided as a service to support NGA operations to automatically detect and extract geospatial features from various data input sources, including imagery and raster maps, to meet the objectives described within the AFE Statement of Objectives (SOO), section 4.0.

2.1 PLANNED ACQUISITION: see attached SOO. This is a RFI which may develop into an RFP at a future date dependent upon responses received to this RFI.

2.2 PERFORMANCE: If a future RFP is released, it is envisioned to have the following period of performance (POP).

2.2.1 PERIOD (POP): 12-month base period, and four (4) 12-month option periods.

2.2.2 LOCATION(S): The primary place of performance for this effort shall be the contractor's facilities. The Government requires a solution that allows for the remote submission of processing jobs via Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) from Government systems to a contractor-provided platform. This platform must process the submitted jobs and return the resulting AFE data to the Government in the required format. Travel to Government facilities, such as NGA Washington (NGA-W) or NGA St. Louis (NGA-STL), shall be on an as-needed basis, as determined and approved by the Government.

Example 3:
7 Proof of Concept Scope
During the challenge, contractor solutions will be run in parallel with CMS's legacy shared systems. This head-to-head approach ensures that performance, accuracy, and reliability can be directly measured against current operations. Contractors must demonstrate that their platforms can adjudicate Part A, Part B and DME claims in near real-time, deliver outputs consistent with legacy results, and provide transparent explanations for any variances. Success will be evaluated based on clear, measurable metrics defined in the POC Metrics table and QASP including accuracy, throughput, interoperability, and stakeholder satisfaction. CMS anticipates that only one solution will advance to production.

PoC scope may include, but is not limited to:
1. Adjudicating large claim volumes quickly and accurately. Accuracy will be measured through both CMS and contractor tools that compare adjudication outcomes and artifacts with the legacy system.
2. Successfully integrating with Tier 1 Systems as described below.
3. Demonstrating user interfaces and operational workflows (including both end-user and administrative functions) relevant to the PoC claim scope including any pre-configured Original Medicare rule sets, workflows, or configuration.
4. Demonstrate the 276/277 X12 transaction flow is happening accurately and in near real-time.

The primary objective of this award is to continue to provide support, maintenance, and enhancements to the TruData Modernization Data Platform. TruData Modernization will become the sole data platform that will form the backbone of FMCSA's IT modernization. FMCSA CTO, in collaboration with OCIO and other FMCSA leaders has set a strategy to build a Data Platform that will include a Data Lake, Data Warehouses and Data Marts that will enable efficient data storage, exchange and analysis for improving the registration, inspection, compliance and enforcement mission functions for FMCSA. The TruData Modernization effort will also streamline data exchanges between mission systems and eliminate redundant capabilities delivered by SAFER, SAFETYNET and other data structures implemented solely for the purpose of information sharing between systems. A continued innovative technical approach is needed to fully implement the data platform for FMCSA and realize the objectives outlined in TruData Modernization Bridge SOO. Specifically: The Contractor shall provide non-personal, IT systems support, staffing controls, program management, and shall deliver services both on and off-site as necessary to execute against all requirements established in the SOO and as set forth by the Government. All development and testing will occur within DOT/FMCSA environments, or instances of environments licensed to DOT/FMCSA, unless explicitly permitted by the COR in writing. Continue the development process with the entire contractor and government team that: develops end-to-end capability in an agile manner; facilitates open communication through regular check-ins and collaborative sessions; and provides clear actors/activities/documents/outcomes.

Describe what services you provide to help transition from our current on premise, Unidata version of Ellucian Colleague to a new SaaS solution.

C.3.4 Development Methodologies
The Contractor shall support a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), a flexible set of design principles applied during system development and integration phases, as specified at the TOs level.

Any SOA-based architecture shall be deployed on a secure, scalable, interoperable, and dynamic platform that provides end-to-end visibility and manageability – from application services through networking components – and supports use across multiple domains.

For HUD-specific TOs, the Contractor shall support HUD efforts in accordance with the following framework and processes:

Visual Innovation Platform (VIP) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM):
 VIP enhances collaboration and decision-making through visual tools (e.g. digital concept boards, etc.).
 PLM serves as a central hub for data, connecting engineering, design, manufacturing, and supply chain teams.
 VIP and PLM work together to integrate workflows and drive digital transformation:
o VIP connects to PLM, pulling real-time product data to create interactive visual workspaces.
o Teams use VIP's visual boards to ideate, review, and make decisions, which then updates the central PLM system.
 This integration bridges strategic vision (VIP) with operational execution (PLM), accelerating innovation and information technology (IT) maturity.

The Contractor shall perform duties consistent with processes defined in the HUD Process Asset Library (PAL), a centralized digital repository that stores and shares process-related resources (templates, guidelines, policies, checklists, lessons learned, and metrics).

For HUD-specific TOs, the Contractor shall follow the HUD PAL, PLM, and VIP processes to ensure compliance with HUD policies, regulations, and quality standards. The Contractor shall use an incremental development methodology such as Agile (e.g. Scrum, Kanban, etc.), unless otherwise specified at the TOs level.

Evaluation Method

The Government will evaluate quotations based on the lowest price technically acceptable criteria. Only the lowest priced offer will be evaluated for Technical Acceptability. Should the lowest priced offer not receive an acceptable technical rating, the process will continue in order of lowest priced offer until the lowest price, technically acceptable offer with acceptable or neutral past performance is identified.

The Government shall evaluate the vendor's quote on a pass/fail basis, assigning ratings of acceptable or unacceptable using the ratings and descriptions outlined as follows:

Rating Description
Acceptable: The quote meets the requirements of the solicitation. The vendor has relevant experience, which has been positively verified by the references provided.
Unacceptable: The vendor does not have relevant experience and/or was not positively verified by the references provided.

Price- The total evaluated price shall be the sum of all Contract Line Item Numbers (CLINs). Quotes will be evaluated to determine whether it is fair and reasonable. The Government shall not award a contract that is not fair and reasonable. Vendors are encouraged to offer discounts beyond their current pricing.

c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

Example 3:
To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

CONTRACTOR REQUIREMENTS
3.0 Technical Requirements/Tasks:
Contractor will come to NDSU Peltier Complex Pilot Mill room facility where the MIAG mill is located. Contractor will:
1. Conduct a site visit for a detailed review and documentation of the existing infrastructure and process flow for the MIAG mill.
2. Perform safety evaluation of the equipment and facility against relevant regulatory (OSHA) standards.
3. Develop preliminary engineering documentation: piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&ID), layout drawings, parts and equipment lists, piping schematics, and detailed remediation punchlist.
4. Assess installation options and infrastructure compatibility, including tie-in locations, pipe routing, utility access, and control integration (if deemed applicable).
5. Solicit budgetary pricing from vendors to inform the purchasing strategy and budgetary planning for Phase II (Detailed engineering, procurement, installation, and recommissioning).

4.0 Deliverable / Schedule:
• USDA-ARS will contact contractor and schedule the site visit at a mutually agreed upon date, generally within 7 days of issuing the service contract.

5.0 Government Furnished:
• ARS will provide the contractor access to the Pilot Mill room located at NDSU Peltier Complex (1300 18th Street North, Fargo, ND) in cooperation with WQL director or ARS-technicians. Access can generally be gained from 6:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday through Friday, excepting federal holidays.

Example 1:
PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

Example 2:
9. FILE TWO – PRICING (Reference Supplies, Services and Prices & Evaluation Factors - LPTA for Award, Price Evaluation)
9.1. File Organization
9.1.1. Table of Contents
9.1.2. List of Tables and Figures
9.1.3. Incremental and Total Price Summary (ATCH 05)
9.1.3.1. Contractor will propose unit prices for each Contract Line Item (CLIN) – IAW Supplies, Services and Prices of this solicitation. Failure to provide prices as stated above might result in rejection of the quote. Total evaluated price will include the base and all option years.

10. FILE THREE – TECHNICAL SOLUTION (Statement of Objectives (SOO) & Evaluation Factors - LPTA for Award)
10.1. File Organization
10.1.1. Table of Contents File III
10.1.2. List of Tables & Figures for File III
10.1.3. Assumptions
10.1.4. Technical Solution/ Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)
10.2. Technical Solution/CSOW
10.2.1. The Contractor will provide a CSOW. The CSOW defines/explains how the contractor will meet the tasks required to fulfill the requirements of the Statement of Objectives (SOO). The Contractor will provide a written response outlining its technical solution to execute the requirement outlined in each paragraph and subparagraph in the SOO. "Yes," "Met," and other ambiguous responses that do not provide an actual technical solution will be deemed unacceptable. The NAFI will evaluate the CSOW as part of Technical Evaluation (Evaluation Factors - LPTA). The CSOW will be incorporated into the contract award.

Example 3:
4. EVALUATION FACTORS
As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The CSOW will include at a minimum a narrative addressing each paragraph in the Statement of Objectives (SOO) (specifically Paragraphs 3-6.12). The narrative will outline the Contractor's complete understanding of the requirement and how the contractor will fulfill all requirements.

The Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW) will be incorporated into the contract at the time of award. See the SOO, for the technical aspects.

BASIS FOR AWARD
This acquisition is being conducted in accordance with FAR 13.5 along with procedures found within FAR 12 and FAR 15. This is a best value acquisition where the source selection process utilized is Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA). Evaluation of quotations will be IAW FAR 52.212-2.
Following the LPTA evaluation process:
The Government will first evaluate price to determine the lowest through highest priced quotations. Only the lowest priced quote will be evaluated for technical acceptability, unless the lowest priced quote is deemed technically unacceptable. If the lowest priced quote is deemed technically unacceptable, the next lowest quote will be considered, and so on until a quoter is identified to be technically acceptable.
Contractors are advised that the Government may not evaluate the technical quotation response of all Contractors under this RFQ.

Example 1:
The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. Automated testing, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines will be central to this framework, enabling quick iterations and reducing certification and Authorization to Operate (ATO) delays. By aligning with modern software practices, Case Development Modernization (CDM) will ensure that value is delivered early and continuously throughout the lifecycle. CDM delivery includes, but is not limited to, the following activities and responsibilities:
• Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

Lee County Government, Parks & Recreation is currently soliciting proposals for Recreation Management Software with real-time internet registration, to support the processes managed by the Parks & Recreation Department, including but not limited to; the daily management of administration tasks, such as program and class reservations, facility reservations, field reservations, memberships, league scheduling, point of sale, picnic reservations, payment processing, customized reporting, exporting of program information for guide creation, ability to upload and store Adobe documents and any related professional services required to implement/train new users on the software. The Recreation Management Software must support industry open standard touch screen computers, bar code scanners, cash drawers, programmable keyboard or keypads, magnetic stripe readers, customer displays, mobile smart phones, tablets, and other peripheral hardware.

- IAS Security and Program Management

Estimated (Base: 6 months + Option: 6 months)

Buchanan and Edwards is uniquely qualified due to:

- Specialized Expertise: Proven experience with IAS SDLC, PRISM upgrades, Oracle DB migration, and USDA-specific interfaces (FMMI, IPP).
- Urgency & Mission Criticality: The current bridge contract awarded during a bid protest period is due to expire 2/28/2026. RFP chunk to analyze:
The State of Washington is in the process of developing specifications for Mainframe COBOL Development support and is requesting input from the vendor community. OFM operates a legacy enterprise core processing platform, which includes AFRS (Agency Financial Reporting System) and CAMS (Capital Asset Management System), built on an IBM z/OS mainframe environment. AFRS has been in production for over four decades and supports mission-critical business accounting functions including but not limited to transaction processing and batch data integrations with internal and external stakeholders. AFRS and CAMS consist primarily of COBOL programs running under CICS for online transaction processing and batch workloads managed via JCL. Data persistence is handled through DB2, VSAM and sequential datasets. Supporting utilities and integrations leverage tools such as SORT, IDCAMS, FTP, and various scheduling and job control frameworks. AFRS and CAMS interface with distributed applications through file transfers and MQ messaging. Over time, AFRS has undergone incremental enhancements to meet evolving business and regulatory requirements. However, significant portions of the codebase remain monolithic and tightly coupled, with very limited documentation and institutional knowledge concentrated among a small number of senior technical resources approaching retirement.

The mainframe environment (AFRS and CAMS) will be replaced by Workday in October 2027-October 2028, via One Washington (OneWA), which is an enterprise-wide business transformation program focused on replacing 1960s-era technology with Workday for finance, procurement, budget, HR, and the payroll processes. We are currently in Phase 1A of the program, focused on replacing the state's core financial systems. To ensure adequate support of the mainframe environment until the Workday go-live, at which time the mainframe will be decommissioned, we are seeking experienced Mainframe COBOL Developers with strong expertise in IBM z/OS environments, CICS, JCL, DB2, and legacy system analysis. The selected resources will play a critical role in maintaining business continuity, implementing changes as required by business or mandated by the legislature, performing root cause analysis, and supporting production incidents while preserving the integrity of the core system.

- IAS System Operations (monitoring, administration, database support)
- IAS/IPP Help Desk Support
- IAS Releases, Upgrades, and Enhancements
- IAS Security and Program Management

Estimated (Base: 6 months + Option: 6 months)

Buchanan and Edwards is uniquely qualified due to:
- Continuity & Risk Mitigation: Retains critical IAS personnel (Program Manager, Functional Leads, Senior Developers) without costly and risky transition.
- Specialized Expertise: Proven experience with IAS SDLC, PRISM upgrades, Oracle DB migration, and USDA-specific interfaces (FMMI, IPP).
- Urgency & Mission Criticality: The current bridge contract awarded during a bid protest period is due to expire 2/28/2026. USDA and OCP have urgent requirements including but not limited to the second release and continued enhancement of the Requirements Planning Tool (RPT) 2.0, that necessitate the continuity of current IAS key personnel to ensure successful execution.

Example 2:
To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Example 3:
Project Scope: The selected vendor will be responsible for implementation of a Cloud-based/SaaS ERP/SIS solution to provide the following services: Must support all Core ERP/SIS functions including but not limited to: Financial Aid – Process Financial Aid Applications, Manage Need Analysis, Track Requirements, Complies with Federal regulations, Funds Management, Budgeting, Package and Award Aid, Disburse Aid, Process Loans, Return Title IV Funds, Manage Federal and Institutional Work Study, Transfer Monitoring, Monitor Satisfactory Academic Progress, Manage Communications, Maintain the College Financing Plan, Self-Service for Financial Aid. Degree Planning and Awarding – Course Catalogs, Requirements substitution and waiver processing. Student Accounts Receivables – Managing Student Accounts, Student Billing and Delinquencies, Manage Exemptions and Contracts, Title IV processing, Accounts Receivable, Collections/Aging, Cash Receipting, Self-Service for Student Accounts Receivable. Financial Information System – GL, Chart of Accounts, Budget Development, Personnel Budgeting, Purchasing, Purchase Orders, Receiving, Reporting, Accounts Payable, Fixed Assets, Research Accounting (Proposals and Grants), Cash Management, Self-Service for Financial Services. Select HRIS Features to Integrate with Paycom (HRIS). Must include data migration and implementation support and customizations. Describe what services you provide to help transition from our current on premise, Unidata version of Ellucian Colleague to a new SaaS solution.

The historical proprietary systems under the Business Support System Program task orders will be decommissioned and removed from the ICE environment, freeing up technical assets and resources.

The incumbent contractor is uniquely positioned to perform this logical follow-on work efficiently by leveraging existing proprietary knowledge, codebases, integrations, security artifacts, and documentation, ensuring continuity and minimizing mission risk during the transition.

As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The CSOW will include at a minimum a narrative addressing each paragraph in the Statement of Objectives (SOO) (specifically Paragraphs 3-6.12). The narrative will outline the Contractor's complete understanding of the requirement and how the contractor will fulfill all requirements.

The Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW) will be incorporated into the contract at the time of award. See the SOO, for the technical aspects.

10.2.1. The Contractor will provide a CSOW. The CSOW defines/explains how the contractor will meet the tasks required to fulfill the requirements of the Statement of Objectives (SOO). The Contractor will provide a written response outlining its technical solution to execute the requirement outlined in each paragraph and subparagraph in the SOO. "Yes," "Met," and other ambiguous responses that do not provide an actual technical solution will be deemed unacceptable. Example 3:
Requirements – Technical: The selected software must meet the following criteria: Content Management System – preference will be given to a mature, proven well-known CMS that supports multiple secure user accounts that enable specific view/report/add/edit permissions. Software should be reliable, secure and robust, in accordance with industry standards and practices. The software must be cloud based (software as a service) platform. Ability to integrate with other software solutions via an API or other methods that would allow the potential for CRM and Web Site interfaces. Software will contain an accurate and complete audit trail that ensures efficient processing. Software must be able to identify and itemize all fees; such as program, rental, transaction, and convenience fees. Must have the ability to provide total program costs to client. Software must be compatible with the County's financial software, Tyler Munis, and post transactions to the General Ledger. Software shall include the ability to implement different security access levels, and elevated admin privileges to appropriate staff. Launch Live Date – Targeted for July 1, 2026. Software must have a comprehensive storage procedure to store all documents and records in a manner that is easily retrievable by County. Software must have cybersecurity protections and company must provide cyberliability insurance coverage.

RFP chunk to analyze:
For code developed under Tasks 1 through 3, the following specifications shall be met: 1. All code shall be covered with unit tests to at least 95% to verify functionality and to guard against regressions from future refactoring, extension, and/or bug fixes. 2. All code shall be supplemented by software test benches that enable simulation and unit testing without the need for hardware. Testing code may be written in Matlab or Python. 3. All code shall be documented by appropriate comments and naming conventions (in addition to Task 2 above.) 4. Proprietary or closed-source software, including FPGA IP cores, shall not be used unless the Contractor has previously verified NIST already owns such IP cores. 5. Code shall be organized to be reviewed by the Government on a regular basis, as specified in the deliverable table (Section IV). The results of the reviews are to be integrated into successive development steps. 6. This code shall be compatible with the existing firmware and custom NIST real-time processor for optical two-way time transfer. 7. All project software being developed shall be maintained in a suitable repository (e.g., GitHub) to allow Government to monitor development progress and provide rapid feedback when changes are needed. 8. The documentation shall include a description of the basic algorithms and their implementation in the DSP or FPGA. The documentation shall also include a basic mathematical description of the algorithm appropriate for publication in a scientific journal.

Beyond these key roles, non-key personnel categories include: Telecommunications Engineer, Cybersecurity Engineer, Systems Administrator, Information System Security Engineer (ISSE), Administrative Assistant, CAD Technician, Radio Frequency Systems Technologist, Information System Security Officer (ISSO), Logistician, Business Process Manager, Purchasing and Materials Coordinator, Project Manager, Configuration Manager, Information Technology Architect, Antenna Specialist, and Software Developer.

1. Description of Services
The Hybrid Architecture and Development for Experimental Systems (HADES) contract is a procurement for prototype and operational satellite ground systems, satellite operations centers (SOCs), and support services. The contractor shall perform satellite ground system and satellite operations center engineering, development, integration, and sustainment, and associated support services primarily at, but not limited to, the Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation (RDT&E) Support Complex (RSC) at Kirtland Air Force Base, Albuquerque, New Mexico. The organization's goal is to accelerate mission design and ground integration, launch and on-orbit operations, ground system tests, and rapid fielding to provide reliable, low-cost access to space in support of the warfighter.

1.1. Background
1.1.1. The Research Development Space and Missile Operations (RDSMO) program provides research, development, and test operations for assigned Department of Defense (DoD) experimental, demonstration, and prototype satellites, space vehicles, satellite ground control systems, tracking systems, and commercial applications of these systems.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

Example 1:
4. EVALUATION FACTORS
As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. 2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

RFP chunk to analyze:
The contractor shall perform satellite ground system and satellite operations center engineering, development, integration, and sustainment, and associated support services primarily at, but not limited to, the Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation (RDT&E) Support Complex (RSC) at Kirtland Air Force Base, Albuquerque, New Mexico. The organization's goal is to accelerate mission design and ground integration, launch and on-orbit operations, ground system tests, and rapid fielding to provide reliable, low-cost access to space in support of the warfighter.

Background
The Research Development Space and Missile Operations (RDSMO) program provides research, development, and test operations for assigned Department of Defense (DoD) experimental, demonstration, and prototype satellites, space vehicles, satellite ground control systems, tracking systems, and commercial applications of these systems. The RDSMO program may also provide these services to approved customers on a cost reimbursable basis.

The RDSMO program is managed by SSC/SD's Prototype Ground Development and Sustainment Branch (SDG) and operations are executed by SSC/SD's Prototype Operations Branch (SDX). Together, these Branches provide the expertise, infrastructure, and processes necessary to manage and execute satellite and ground system engineering and operations to include scientific, technology demonstration, developmental, and prototype systems, while enabling rapid migration of space capabilities to the warfighter.

The contractor is a mission partner with the Government, customers, Federally Funded Research and Development Centers (FFRDCs), and associate contractors. The RSC provides access to a low-cost, reliable ground system and resources for 24x7 spacecraft operations to support the scientific and space acquisition communities. The satellite ground system, satellite operations centers, and support services being procured through this contract shall occur primarily, but not solely, at the following locations: Kirtland Air Force Base, NM.

A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

FAR Part 19.1306(a) specify when a sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is appropriate. This acquisition meets those circumstances, as follows:
a. The contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns; Market research identified DH Technologies, LLC as the only HUBZone small business authorized distributor for these items;
b. The anticipated price of the contract, including options, will not exceed $6.5 million for a requirement within the NAICS codes for manufacturing or $4.5 million for a requirement, within any other NAICS code; the applicable NAICS code is 541519 so the $4.5 million limitation applies. As stated above, the estimated dollar value is $532,194 for the base year;
c. This is a new requirement that is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under the provisions of FAR subpart 19.8. No performance by an equal 8(a) participant was identified during market research;
d. The HUBZone small business concern has been determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance, DH Technologies, LLC has a history of successful performance on previous contracts for these items.

Notice of Price Evaluation preference for HUBZone Small Business Concerns (Oct 2022)
(a) Evaluation preference.
(1) Offers will be evaluated by adding a factor of 10 percent to the price of all offers, except-
(i) Offers from HUBZone small business concerns that have not waived the evaluation preference; and
(ii) Otherwise successful offers from small business concerns.
(2) The factor of 10 percent shall be applied on a line item basis or to any group of items on which award may be made. Other evaluation factors described in the solicitation shall be applied before application of the factor.
(3) When the two highest rated offerors are a HUBZone small business concern and a large business, and the evaluated offer of the HUBZone small business concern is equal to the evaluated offer of the large business after considering the price evaluation preference, award will be made to the HUBZone small business concern.
(b) Waiver of evaluation preference. A HUBZone small business concern may elect to waive the evaluation preference, in which case the factor will be added to its offer for evaluation purposes.

1. Program Objectives
The HADES acquisition is for a new, single Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract for follow-on services supporting the Space Systems Command System Delta 89/Innovation and Prototyping Division Ground (SDG) at Kirtland Air Force Base, NM. This contract will provide engineering, development, integration, and sustainment services supporting the United States Air Force's (USAF) Research and Development Space and Missile Operations (RDSMO) Ground System Enterprise (GSE).
The SSC/SDG mission is to operate and sustain an affordable GSE for Research & Development and demonstration missions, develop innovative solutions to reduce Operations & Maintenance (O&M) costs, provide a test bed to prove out emerging concepts, and remain a viable choice for all new ground Command and Control (C2) programs.
SSC/SDG provides a collaborative partnership among developmental, test and operational units to seamlessly develop and rapidly field new capabilities, standardize satellite operations and exploit core competencies and strengths.
The GSE represents the entire ground infrastructure that falls under the RDSMO program. The primary components of the GSE include, but are not limited to, two instances of physical infrastructure hosting fully virtualized instances of the core and mission unique ground system software supporting multiple missions, the core Common User Equipment (CUE) and communications equipment (COMM), and facilities that fall under the RDSMO program.

4. EVALUATION FACTORS
As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The CSOW will include at a minimum a narrative addressing each paragraph in the Statement of Objectives (SOO) (specifically Paragraphs 3-6.12). The narrative will outline the Contractor's complete understanding of the requirement and how the contractor will fulfill all requirements.

The Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW) will be incorporated into the contract at the time of award. See the SOO, for the technical aspects.

To be considered for this work, the contractor must be capable of providing an MSP&A integration / innovation lab, supporting approximately 60 personnel, in the Colorado Springs, CO area that is approved (or will be within the current government fiscal year) to operate up to TS/SCI and SAP levels. The capabilities at this facility should allow multiple vendors to collaborate on analytical objectives supporting the customer(s).

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

A sole source HUBZone small business set-aside is justified under 10 USC 2304(c)(5), FAR 6.302-5, authorized or required by statute.

b. Example 1:
PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

Example 3:
PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

The Government intends to utilize oral proposals to evaluate Factor 1, Technical/Management Approach. The presentation shall be limited to 36 PowerPoint slides, which shall be provided to the Government with proposal submission. For an offeror's proposal to be rated Acceptable for Factor 1, Technical/Management Approach, each of the following subfactors must be rated Acceptable. If any of the subfactors under Factor 1 are rated unacceptable, the offeror will not be further evaluated and is not eligible for award.

(a) Subfactor 1: Core Service Capability & Performance, PWS Paragraphs: 6.2 and 6.2.2
The Government will evaluate the offeror's proposal to determine their ability to perform the core service capability and performance outlined in PWS Section 6.2 and 6.2.2. This factor will be rated as either "Acceptable" or "Unacceptable" basis. To be considered acceptable, the offeror's proposal must meet all of the minimum requirements set forth below.

The offeror must demonstrate the ability to:
1. Deliver and sustain the core isolation capability for up to 3.4 million users, including the technical methodology to isolate all Internet code execution while ensuring page load times are no more than 5 seconds and round-trip latency is no more than 100ms (PWS 6.2).
2. Integrate the CBII platform with the DISN Joint Infrastructure (DJI) security stack, including Web Content Filtering (WCF) and Enterprise Break & Inspect (EBI), ensuring all required traffic is inspected without performance degradation performance (PWS 6.2).

This contract shall not include any terms inconsistent with Federal law. (3) Law and disputes. This contract is governed by Federal law.
